meta.description: Structured lesson index for closure, owner handoff, failure signatures, and anti-pattern retrieval.

lessons.title: Source leak closure
lessons.better_question: What is the shortest source-guided path from current control to flag, secret, config, or admin-only state?
lessons.stop_rule: Do not return to broad route discovery until the top two source-guided closure probes fail or are falsified.
related_pattern_queries: web source backward slice sink config secret route
web debug config source flag recovery
lessons.closure_owner_hint: the surface that owns source-guided sink-to-flag completion

lessons.title: File read closure
lessons.better_question: What is the cheapest highest-probability file set for this runtime, user, and app root?
lessons.stop_rule: After two low-probability path mutations with no new differential, stop broad path guessing.
related_pattern_queries: web file read lfi config env app root
web include read path closure
lessons.budget_penalty: shrink path-guess budget after two flat low-probability reads
lessons.closure_owner_hint: the surface controlling filesystem read and target path ranking

lessons.title: Admin/session closure
lessons.better_question: Which privileged surface is most likely to reveal the flag or its storage location immediately?
lessons.stop_rule: Do not drop back into generic exploitation until the top privileged data surfaces are tested.
related_pattern_queries: web admin route export debug logs hidden api
lessons.closure_owner_hint: the owner that controls privileged route and object closure

lessons.title: SSRF/internal closure
lessons.better_question: Has SSRF already solved the information problem, making closure more valuable than further enumeration?
lessons.stop_rule: After source or config is already acquired, do not keep broad SSRF enumeration unless closure probes fail.
related_pattern_queries: web ssrf internal source config metadata admin route
lessons.budget_penalty: freeze broad SSRF enumeration once source or config is already acquired

lessons.title: DB read closure
lessons.better_question: Which tables, collections, or fields are most likely to contain the flag, secret, admin token, or route clue?
lessons.stop_rule: Do not keep full-database exploration once a likely flag-bearing structure exists.
related_pattern_queries: web db read schema secret config admin token hidden object

lessons.title: File write closure
lessons.better_question: What is the cheapest adapter that converts this write into readback, include, render, execution, or privileged state?
lessons.stop_rule: After a viable adapter exists, freeze unrelated discovery until the top two closure adapters fail.
promote_if: write path and trigger are understood
demote_if: new discovery continues despite viable adapters
related_pattern_queries: web file write upload include render filter adapter

lessons.title: Deserialization closure
lessons.better_question: What is the cheapest confirmed gadget effect that gets me to read, write, or privileged state with the current sink?
lessons.stop_rule: Do not keep gadget hunting when one confirmed gadget already composes into a strong closure path.
demote_if: gadget search expands without closure movement
related_pattern_queries: web deserialization gadget read write invoke render

lessons.title: Template injection closure
lessons.better_question: What is the smallest template-side read path that reaches config, source, env, filesystem, or privileged state?
lessons.stop_rule: Read beats shell when a direct flag/config/source path exists.
demote_if: payload cleverness increases without read closure
related_pattern_queries: web ssti template env config source read path

lessons.title: JWT/token forge closure
lessons.better_question: Which privileged route or object becomes reachable once the forged token is used?
lessons.stop_rule: After forgeability is confirmed, token theory is lower priority than privileged route closure.
promote_if: forged or stronger token is usable
related_pattern_queries: web jwt role forge admin route privileged api

lessons.title: Java actuator closure
lessons.better_question: Which actuator/config output most directly reveals credentials, file paths, or route enablement?
lessons.stop_rule: Do not keep broad actuator curiosity once a secret-bearing or route-enabling output is visible.
related_pattern_queries: java spring actuator env mappings secret credential
lessons.owner_flip_trigger: Java explains the sink, config path, or privileged route better than Web transport

lessons.title: Rev checker recovery closure
lessons.better_question: What exact unknown still blocks reconstruction, and do I need more reversing or just a solver now?
lessons.stop_rule: Once the checker is modeled, more browsing is lower value than solver completion.
demote_if: code browsing continues without unknown reduction
related_pattern_queries: reverse checker solver equation lookup table validation logic

lessons.title: Crypto oracle to plaintext closure
lessons.better_question: What is the minimal remaining unknown between the current primitive and a decodable or forgeable final artifact?
lessons.stop_rule: Once the oracle or weakness is real, stop broad reconnaissance and finish the attack.
demote_if: side facts accumulate without solve completion
related_pattern_queries: crypto oracle plaintext partial key forgery solver

lessons.title: Pwn leak to win closure
lessons.better_question: What exact blocker remains between the current primitive and stable win or read-flag behavior?
lessons.stop_rule: Do not reopen exploit-family discovery when the branch is already leak or control complete enough to finish.
demote_if: new exploit families distract from blocker resolution
related_pattern_queries: pwn leak orw gadget base flag read stabilization

lessons.title: Web to Java handoff
lessons.better_question: Does HTTP still own the problem, or is it only transport for a Java-controlled sink or closure path?
lessons.stop_rule: No co-primary owners when Java clearly owns the sink or closure blocker.
promote_if: java explains sink, oracle, or closure blocker
demote_if: web remains owner only because it is visible
related_pattern_queries: web java spring actuator stacktrace route sink handoff
lessons.owner_flip_trigger: Java now owns sink semantics or closure blocker

lessons.title: Web to Rev handoff
lessons.better_question: Is HTTP only transport now, while the actual sink, transform, or trust boundary is owned by artifact logic?
lessons.stop_rule: Do not keep Web as owner when artifact logic defines the real primitive.
promote_if: artifact explains semantics better than live request mutation
demote_if: web mutation continues without semantic gain
related_pattern_queries: web reverse apk binary client logic token validation handoff
lessons.owner_flip_trigger: Artifact logic now explains token, validation, or transform semantics

lessons.title: Web to Crypto handoff
lessons.better_question: Is the main uncertainty now route behavior, or cryptographic semantics?
lessons.stop_rule: If crypto owns the next decisive probe, hand off instead of keeping Web primary by habit.
promote_if: next decisive step depends on crypto modeling
demote_if: web mutation dominates despite crypto-owned uncertainty
related_pattern_queries: web crypto token oracle signature verification handoff
lessons.owner_flip_trigger: Crypto semantics now own the next decisive probe

lessons.title: Web to Forensics handoff
lessons.better_question: Is the next best differential on the live service, or inside the provided artifact?
lessons.stop_rule: Do not keep fuzzing the service when the attachment itself is the stronger clue source.
promote_if: artifact analysis is likely higher-value than web mutation
demote_if: service probing continues despite richer artifact clues
related_pattern_queries: web forensics artifact archive image embedded clue handoff
lessons.owner_flip_trigger: Artifact evidence is now more informative than live Web mutation

lessons.title: Medium-value primitive drift
lessons.better_question: Is this branch actually moving closer to flag, source, database, or privileged state?
lessons.stop_rule: Medium-value branches need a two-step route to higher-value state or they are downgraded.
promote_if: clear two-step path to flag or secret appears
demote_if: two same-family probes produce no progress
related_pattern_queries: web reflection xss open redirect no secret route medium value
lessons.budget_penalty: downgrade medium-value branch after two no-progress probes

lessons.title: Source not prioritized
signals: black-box branch continues despite cheap source path
lessons.better_question: Is source acquisition cheaper than the current branch complexity?
lessons.stop_rule: Do not deepen complex medium-value branches while a cheap source/data surface is still untested.
demote_if: black-box branch dominates despite available source
related_pattern_queries: web git leak backup file cheap source source-first
lessons.budget_penalty: cap black-box branch until cheap source surfaces are resolved

lessons.title: Flat differential branch not killed
signals: payload variance mistaken for hypothesis variance
lessons.better_question: What orthogonal variable would change the actual hypothesis rather than just the payload string?
lessons.stop_rule: Three no-differential same-family variants is a stale branch.
related_pattern_queries: same family no new oracle flat differential endpoint calibration
lessons.budget_penalty: kill same-family branch after repeated flat differentials

lessons.title: Owner should switch earlier
signals: transport visibility mistaken for sink ownership
lessons.better_question: Which surface now explains the sink, parser, or closure blocker best?
lessons.stop_rule: No co-primary owners; hand off when support owns sink or closure understanding.
promote_if: supporting surface explains sink or blocker better
demote_if: current owner survives only by momentum
lessons.owner_flip_trigger: supporting surface now explains sink or closure blocker better than current owner

lessons.title: Primitive confirmed, no closure model
lessons.better_question: Why is the branch still exploring if a plausible closure queue already exists?
lessons.stop_rule: After a high-value primitive, unrelated bug-family exploration is suspended until the top closure probes fail.
lessons.budget_penalty: freeze unrelated discovery until top closure probes fail

lessons.title: Repeated proc/environment enumeration
lessons.better_question: What stronger semantic, parser, or closure probe now has higher information value than another proc path guess?
lessons.stop_rule: Two no-progress environment or proc probes means the family is frozen until new evidence appears.
promote_if: direct source, flag, or secret evidence appears
demote_if: two no-differential proc probes already failed
related_pattern_queries: semantic mismatch parser differential content type path normalization
lessons.budget_penalty: freeze proc/environment family after two no-progress probes

lessons.title: Reflection without secret route
lessons.better_question: Where is the secret-bearing browser state, privileged reader, or exfil route?
lessons.stop_rule: Without a secret route, reflection stays medium-value at best.
promote_if: bot, secret endpoint, or DOM-held secret appears
lessons.budget_penalty: reflection family gets minimal budget without secret route

lessons.title: SSRF after source acquisition
lessons.better_question: What existing source or config clue should now drive closure?
lessons.stop_rule: If source or config is already acquired, SSRF exploration must justify a concrete missing dependency.
promote_if: closure probes fail and a missing internal dependency is named
lessons.budget_penalty: stop broad SSRF enumeration after source/config acquisition

lessons.title: JWT decode without trust boundary
lessons.better_question: Where is the actual trust boundary that would make this token exploitable?
lessons.stop_rule: Decodability is not exploitability; without trust-boundary evidence, token curiosity is downgraded.
promote_if: weak secret, kid/jku/jwk misuse, or role-sensitive endpoint appears
demote_if: decode continues to dominate despite no exploitability path
lessons.budget_penalty: token curiosity gets low budget until trust-boundary evidence appears

lessons.title: RCE obsession when read path exists
lessons.better_question: Why is execution worth more than the current direct read path to flag, config, or source?
lessons.stop_rule: Read beats shell when it already reaches the flag path more cheaply.
promote_if: direct read paths are blocked and execution is actually required
demote_if: execution dominates despite viable read closure
lessons.budget_penalty: execution obsession is downgraded when a cheaper read path exists

lessons.title: JWT decode without trust boundary (hard-mode)
lessons.better_question: Which exact server-side trust boundary makes this token exploitable, and can one harmless mutation prove significance?
lessons.stop_rule: Do not spend hard-mode budget on JWT mutation families until token class, validation path, and claim/key-location significance are classified.
demote_if: source shows server ignores client claims
related_pattern_queries: web jwt trust boundary claim significance kid jku role authorization
lessons.budget_penalty: cap JWT curiosity until a trust-boundary differential exists

lessons.title: Category failure signatures backlog
lessons.better_question: Which recurring failure signature deserves a structured stop rule before the next hard challenge?
lessons.stop_rule: Do not treat backlog bullets as tactical advice until converted into a structured lesson with triggers and first probes.
related_pattern_queries: ctf failure signatures backlog pwn rev crypto web anti-pattern

lessons.title: Coppersmith without bounds
lessons.better_question: What is the exact polynomial, unknown variable, modulus, and bound inequality that makes Coppersmith applicable?
lessons.stop_rule: No lattice implementation until polynomial form and small-root bound assumptions are explicitly checked against challenge sizes.
related_pattern_queries: crypto coppersmith small root bounds rsa lattice polynomial
lessons.budget_penalty: block Sage/lattice coding until precondition table exists

lessons.title: Custom algebra before family elimination
lessons.better_question: Which crypto family is actually supported by parameters/oracle evidence, and what cheapest test distinguishes it from simpler families?
lessons.stop_rule: Do not start custom algebra before normalizing parameters and eliminating simpler families with one discriminating probe each.
related_pattern_queries: crypto family precondition matrix oracle normalization rsa attacks
lessons.budget_penalty: force family/precondition matrix before advanced algebra

lessons.title: Transcript not normalized before attack coding
lessons.better_question: What canonical parser/serializer defines each field, byte order, encoding, and transcript order consumed by the attack?
lessons.stop_rule: No cryptanalytic implementation until all values are normalized into a single canonical representation and round-tripped where possible.
related_pattern_queries: crypto transcript normalization parser serializer encoding byte int
lessons.budget_penalty: freeze attack coding until transcript normalization is complete

lessons.title: Memory sweep without target process
lessons.better_question: Which process or artifact family is the highest-probability owner of the secret/session/credential, and what focused extraction proves it?
lessons.stop_rule: Do not sweep arbitrary processes until one anchor artifact and ranked target process are selected.
related_pattern_queries: forensics memory image volatility process ranking credential browser session
lessons.budget_penalty: require target process selection before deep dumping

lessons.title: Packet reading before stream ranking
lessons.better_question: Which reconstructed conversations are most likely to contain credentials, sessions, files, or exfil objects?
lessons.stop_rule: Rank conversations by protocol, byte volume, and auth/object clues before reading individual packets deeply.
related_pattern_queries: forensics pcap stream ranking tcp http objects credentials
lessons.budget_penalty: block packet-by-packet reading until stream ranking exists

lessons.title: Partial control treated as full RCE
lessons.better_question: What exact field is controlled, and what first reusable read/write/control primitive can that field promote into?
lessons.stop_rule: Do not design full RCE until partial control is promoted into a proven read, write, leak, or control-flow primitive.
related_pattern_queries: pwn partial overwrite primitive ladder length field pointer control
lessons.budget_penalty: force primitive-ladder proof before endgame ROP/shell

lessons.title: Shell obsession before easier flag-read closure
lessons.better_question: Is a direct ORW/read-flag chain cheaper and more stable than obtaining an interactive shell?
lessons.stop_rule: When shell is blocked/unstable and a read path is plausible, prioritize direct flag-read or ORW closure over shell variants.
related_pattern_queries: pwn seccomp ORW flag read ret2libc one_gadget
lessons.budget_penalty: cap shell attempts once ORW/read-flag route is plausible

lessons.title: Version roulette before route gating
lessons.better_question: Which exploit assumptions are version-sensitive, and what cheapest clue constrains libc/allocator/loader before route commitment?
lessons.stop_rule: Do not rotate versions or gadgets before obtaining one constraining leak, fingerprint, or provided-environment clue.
demote_if: family switch from one failed gadget
related_pattern_queries: pwn libc version leak build-id allocator fingerprint heap glibc
lessons.budget_penalty: require version gate clue before version-sensitive route deepening

lessons.title: Patching before stable observation point
lessons.better_question: Where is the smallest barrier, and what stable post-barrier code/memory view can be captured before patching?
lessons.stop_rule: Do not broadly patch control flow before localizing the barrier and recording one stable post-barrier observation.
related_pattern_queries: reverse anti-debug unpack stable observation patching checker
lessons.budget_penalty: capture stable observation before broad patching

lessons.title: Symbolic escalation before state-machine extraction
lessons.better_question: What is the minimal real checker/state machine, and which state variables are touched by one controlled input trace?
lessons.stop_rule: Do not escalate to symbolic solving until input boundary, comparison boundary, and minimal state variables are extracted.
related_pattern_queries: reverse checker state machine z3 symbolic path explosion VM
lessons.budget_penalty: block symbolic escalation until state extraction is complete

lessons.title: Table constants read without forward model
lessons.better_question: Can one extracted table round predict an observed state change or comparison for a controlled input?
lessons.stop_rule: Do not invert tables or build solvers until one forward transform step is verified on the real checker path.
related_pattern_queries: reverse lookup table constants forward model controlled input checker
lessons.budget_penalty: require verified forward model before inverse solver

lessons.title: Hidden semantic carrier missed
signals: data staged but not consumed in .text
lessons.better_question: What hidden semantic carrier could still consume the staged data, and has a metadata/unwind pass actually ruled that out?
lessons.stop_rule: Do not conclude checker removal or underconstraint until `.eh_frame` / `.debug_frame` / `.gcc_except_table` and `DW_CFA_val_expression` / `DW_OP_*` checks are negative.
promote_if: frame or patch title hints exist
demote_if: staged data is fully explained in normal .text
related_pattern_queries: reverse eh_frame debug_frame dwarf unwind metadata hidden consumer exception path
lessons.budget_penalty: require one hidden-semantic pass before concluding logic removal

lessons.title: Two-stage ret2libc closure
lessons.better_question: What is the smallest first-stage leak-and-return chain that proves a stable base before final execution?
lessons.stop_rule: Do not rotate libc guesses, gadgets, or one_gadget candidates before one stable first-stage leak succeeds.
related_pattern_queries: pwn ret2libc two stage leak clean return base calculation
lessons.closure_owner_hint: the pwn branch already holding control and leak recovery

lessons.title: ORW/seccomp closure
lessons.better_question: Which allowed syscall sequence reaches the flag fastest with the current primitive?
lessons.stop_rule: Do not continue shell-first mutation once seccomp evidence points to ORW or file-read closure.
related_pattern_queries: pwn seccomp orw open read write syscall allowed shell blocked
lessons.closure_owner_hint: the pwn branch holding the current memory-corruption primitive

lessons.title: movaps/alignment crash treated as gadget luck
lessons.better_question: Would one clean stack-alignment fix explain the crash before any libc or gadget rotation?
lessons.stop_rule: Test one alignment fix before changing libc, gadget family, or one_gadget candidates.
related_pattern_queries: pwn movaps stack alignment ret2libc wrong libc prompt sync
lessons.budget_penalty: freeze gadget/libc roulette until one alignment test is tried

lessons.title: Heap technique before menu state reduction
lessons.better_question: What exact menu state, allocator version, and concrete heap primitive are proven before choosing a named heap route?
lessons.stop_rule: Do not try named heap techniques until the menu state table, allocator version, and one concrete primitive are verified.
related_pattern_queries: heap menu add delete edit show allocator version primitive proof
lessons.budget_penalty: block named heap technique escalation until menu state is reduced

lessons.title: Blind remote mutation before local oracle
lessons.better_question: What is the smallest local or scripted oracle that proves the current hypothesis before more remote mutation?
lessons.stop_rule: Do not continue blind remote mutation after two flat attempts without a new observation or stronger local/runtime model.
related_pattern_queries: pwn remote drift local works remote fails prompt sync libc mismatch timeout
lessons.budget_penalty: shrink remote retry budget until a local or scripted oracle exists

lessons.title: Java dependency without reachability
lessons.better_question: Which request reaches this dependency-controlled parser or sink, with what controlled bytes and what oracle?
lessons.stop_rule: Do not run gadget or CVE payloads until route, config, controlled data shape, and oracle are proven.
related_pattern_queries: java dependency without reachability route parser config gate oracle

lessons.title: Java full decompile third-party waste
lessons.better_question: Which app-owned controller, service, mapper, parser, or security class is closest to a route, sink, or flag path?
lessons.stop_rule: Do not full-decompile third-party libraries before app class map, config map, and selected bytecode hints exist.
related_pattern_queries: java archive map bytecode hints app classes before third party decompile

lessons.title: Java template engine not confirmed
lessons.better_question: Does controlled input reach template name resolution, expression parsing, fragment selection, or only escaped model data?
lessons.stop_rule: After two harmless expression markers produce no engine-specific differential, stop template payload variants.
related_pattern_queries: java template engine confirmed route expression parsing harmless marker

lessons.title: Java upload without served path proof
lessons.better_question: Where is the file stored, is the path user-controlled, is it served or reloaded, and can a canary be read back?
lessons.stop_rule: Do not attempt JSP shells or overwrites until canary create, readback, and served behavior are proven.
related_pattern_queries: java upload served path canary readback jsp static resources

lessons.title: Java auth filter before payload
lessons.better_question: Which identity, session, path, or method reaches the handler and which filter or interceptor blocks it?
lessons.stop_rule: If 401, 403, or 302 dominates, stop payload mutation and build a route-auth table.
related_pattern_queries: java auth filter spring security shiro route auth table path normalization

lessons.title: Java boot failure needs local harness
lessons.better_question: Can the target controller, service, mapper, or parser method be isolated with mocks to prove source-to-sink and oracle?
lessons.stop_rule: Do not spend more than a short setup budget fixing full app boot before writing a harness plan for the top sink.
related_pattern_queries: java boot fail local harness verifier mock controller service parser sink
